sectiondef.header: Inspector References — Overhead UI (world-space canvas children)

briefdescription.para: Label displaying machine ID, e.g. "M3".
Status text displaying current state, e.g. "IDLE".
Slider showing operation completion percentage.
Text showing queue depth, e.g. "Q: 4".
Green.
Yellow.
Red.
Blue.
Colour used during the decision-point flash.
Duration of the flash in seconds.
Per-instance material so colour changes don't leak.
Machine index inside the Taillard instance.
Current visual state.
Number of jobs visually queued at this machine.
Total dispatching decisions made at this machine.
Read-only view of the history log.
Creates a per-instance material clone so SetState colour changes are isolated to this machine, and hides the progress bar.
Drives the fallback real-time progress bar each frame when active.
Cleans up the cloned material to avoid memory leaks.
Initialises and shows the progress bar for a new operation.

parameterdescription.para: Simulation time when the operation begins.
Total duration of the operation in sim-time units.

briefdescription.para: Stops tracking progress and hides the progress bar.
Fallback progress update using Unity real-time.

detaileddescription.para: This path is only hit when UpdateProgress is NOT being called by the bridge. Override with sim time for accurate display.

briefdescription.para: Shows or hides the progress bar GameObject.

parameterdescription.para: true to show; false to hide.

briefdescription.para: Drives the progress bar from an external simulation time. The SimulationBridge should call this each frame with the current simulation clock value.
Snaps all queued job visuals to their correct slot positions.

detaileddescription.para: Currently snaps instantly. Replace with a tween or coroutine for polish.

briefdescription.para: Updates the queue-length UI label to reflect the current count.
Adds a job visual to this machine's queue.

parameterdescription.para: The GameObject representing the queued job.

simplesect.para: The world-space position the job should move to.

briefdescription.para: Removes a specific job from the queue (it is being processed or rerouted). Remaining jobs shift forward to fill the gap.
Gets the world-space position of the "processing" slot where the active job sits while the machine is busy.
Gets the world-space position for the Nth queued job slot.
Coroutine that sets the material to the flash colour, waits, then restores the state-based colour.

memberdef.argsstring: (float simTime, int[] queuedJobIds, int chosenJobId, string ruleName, bool flash=true)
memberdef.name: RecordDecisionPoint

briefdescription.para: Records a dispatching decision at this machine and optionally flashes the visual. Called when the DES makes a dispatching decision (i.e., queue is non-empty when the machine becomes free).

parameterdescription.para: IDs of jobs in the queue at decision time.
The job the dispatching rule selected.
If true, briefly flashes the machine white.

briefdescription.para: Triggers a brief white flash on the machine to visually mark a dispatching decision.
Maps a MachineState to its corresponding display colour.

simplesect.para: The Color assigned to the given state.

briefdescription.para: Appends a timestamped message to the history log.
One-time setup after instantiation. Called by FactoryLayoutManager.

parameterdescription.para: Reference to the core DES Machine object (Scheduling.Core.Machine). Stored as System.Object to avoid a compile-time dependency.

briefdescription.para: Transitions to a new visual state. Updates colour, status label, and progress bar visibility.
Begins an operation — sets state to MachineState.Busy and starts the progress bar. Called by SimulationBridge on OperationStarted events.

parameterdescription.para: DES simulation start time of the operation.

briefdescription.para: Completes the current operation — sets state to MachineState.Idle. Called by SimulationBridge on OperationCompleted events.
Resets all runtime state for a new episode. Called between episodes.
Handle to the active flash coroutine, allowing cancellation of overlapping flashes.
Reference to the core DES Machine object, wired by SimulationBridge.

detaileddescription.para: Stored as System.Object so this file compiles without a hard dependency on the Core assembly. Cast to your Scheduling.Core.Machine when you wire it up.

briefdescription.para: History log entries for the machine inspector (Step 9).
Gets the core DES Machine reference.
Machine operating states — mirrors the core DES Machine states.

detaileddescription.para: Visual representation of a single machine on the factory floor.
Attach this to the MachinePrefab cube alongside a MeshRenderer and a child world-space Canvas for the overhead UI.
